hypotheses.statement: Post-2022 SPY 0DTE ORB debit verticals beat SPY buy-and-hold risk-adjusted without conditioning on macro or regime state.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Opening-range breakouts may reflect early order-flow imbalance after the 0DTE listing expansion.
testable_predictions: Unconditional ORB returns are positive after implementable costs.
OOS risk-adjusted return beats same-trade-calendar SPY.
The effect is not dependent on removing macro or stress regimes.
validation_criteria: E2 requires MinTRL/PSR versus SR0=0 and same-trade-calendar SPY.
E2 requires implementable PnL, big-day survival, regime coverage, and benchmark drawdown comparison.
falsification_criteria: Unconditional OOS implementable PnL is negative with no validation path versus benchmark null.
Mechanism autopsy shows unconditional pooling mixes incompatible macro/stress mechanisms.

decision_log.notes: Unconditional H-A1 is retired; H-A2 is the allowed resurrection with a new macro-conditioned prediction.

hypotheses.statement: The ORB edge, if it exists, is macro-conditioned: excluding high-importance scheduled macro days should improve implementable risk-adjusted OOS returns because non-macro breakout days reflect cleaner order-flow imbalance.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: On scheduled macro days, the opening range can be dominated by anticipatory positioning and post-release repricing rather than persistent intraday imbalance.
testable_predictions: Macro-day trades remain weak or negative in fresh data.
Retained no-macro trades remain positive after cost and big-day removal.
The effect is not concentrated in one VIX/trend bucket unless the hypothesis is explicitly scope-restricted.
validation_criteria: Use untouched validation data or DSR accounting for the inherited 9-trial M5.5 search.
Pass MinTRL/PSR against SR0=0 and same-trade-calendar SPY.
Use implementable PnL, big-day dependency, and regime coverage or explicit scope restriction.
falsification_criteria: Fresh retained no-macro trades have implementable PnL <= 0 after reaching MinTRL_falsify.
Macro-day/no-macro mechanism reverses in fresh data.

scope_restrictions.rationale: Two independent stress windows produced no H-A2 candidates after complete or validated market-data coverage. High-VIX silence is treated as a survival guardrail and an identification boundary, not profitability evidence.
scope_restrictions.operational_guardrail: No H-A2 trade may be opened when prior-close VIX is 25 or higher. High-importance macro days remain excluded independently.

No additional high-VIX purchase for the current H-A2 rule unless a new hypothesis explicitly changes the scope.

Aug 2024 and Oct 2022 independently produced zero H-A2 candidates in stress/high-VIX conditions. H-A2 is now formally restricted to prior-close VIX <25 with high-importance macro days excluded. VIX >=25 is blocked/out-of-scope; silence there may be described as survival behavior but never as profitability or E2 evidence.

The Section 5 plan proposes 20 untouched dates across the two in-scope VIX buckets with a $12.601188 approval ceiling. No live metadata call or purchase occurred. User approval, live cost refresh, and selected-key guard refresh are required before purchase.

Code inspection before parsing fresh OOS showed that proxy_5m.directional_followthrough_to_close_pct uses the 15:45 close although prior artifacts described it as 09:35-known. Threshold-0.001 selection evidence is lookahead-contaminated and cannot support an edge or deployment claim.

The run found 6 retrospective candidates, but Databento OHLCV-1m timestamps the interval start: the 09:35 bar close is available around 09:36 while replay used 09:35 option quotes. Mechanical PnL and derived PSR/MinTRL are invalid for inference. H-A2 remains active but the exact 09:35 question is unanswered; stop expansion until a truly observable 09:35 rule or explicit 09:36 rule is preregistered on untouched data.

hypotheses.statement: The tested current-account, always-eligible capped-risk put ratio spread template is feasible and profitable under $300 allocation and $20 risk budget on a $1,000 account.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Selling short-dated downside skew may have premium edge if tail risk is capped by a protective wing, but the tested H-B1 form did not condition entries on market regime or structure suitability.
testable_predictions: A meaningful share of trades fits the $300 allocation and $20 risk budget.
Implementable PnL is not structurally negative after costs.
validation_criteria: Defined-risk spread construction.
Small-account sizing feasibility.
Implementable PnL and tail-risk metrics.
falsification_criteria: Zero trades fit the current sizing rule.
Implementable PnL remains materially negative under the tested current-account assumptions.
required_data: Existing M4.2 Sub-System B feasibility report.

mintrl_falsify.notes: The feasibility blocker is deterministic: 0 trades fit sizing.

evidence.conclusion: เนเธกเนเธเนเธฒเธ

decision_log.notes: Current small-account H-B1 is killed; H-B2 tests the structure at larger simulated scale.

User challenged whether the failed test asked the right question. H-B1 falsifies the current-account / effectively always-eligible framing, not the entire capped put ratio spread family. A future Sub-System B branch must ask which market regimes, volatility/skew states, or filters make the structure suitable, rather than assuming it should work every day.

hypotheses.statement: The put-ratio-with-wing structure may have positive expectancy at simulated $10k to $25k scale only under appropriate market regimes or entry filters where strike granularity, premium-to-account ratios, volatility/skew state, and tail-risk context are favorable.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: If short-dated downside skew is overpriced, a capped-risk structure may monetize it at sufficient account size while preserving survival through the protective wing. The current grid may have failed because it treated structure eligibility too broadly, not necessarily because the structure has no conditional use.
testable_predictions: Cost drag is not the dominant share of gross edge at all wing widths.
Implementable PnL and ES95-adjusted return are not negative at both simulated scales.
Results are not dependent on fractional contracts or hidden undefined risk.
A pre-registered regime/suitability filter can identify conditions where the structure's tail-risk and skew-premium tradeoff is economically coherent.
validation_criteria: Pre-register $10k and $25k account sizes.
Pre-register wing grid and sizing rule.
Pre-register any regime/suitability filter before rerun; do not add filters after seeing OOS PnL.
Record search log and DSR or DSR blocker.
Report implementable PnL, ES95, drawdown, cost drag, and sample labels.
falsification_criteria: Implementable PnL and ES95-adjusted return are negative at both simulated scales with adequate falsification sample under the pre-registered suitability scope.
Cost drag exceeds 60% of gross at all wing widths.
No logically justified volatility/skew/regime filter can be specified without OOS fitting.
required_data: Already-cached Sub-System B data from Mar-Dec 2023 and Jan-Dec 2024.
H-B2 pre-registration manifest and simulated-scale diagnostic are complete with no new paid data.
Any H-B2 reopening requires a new pre-registered mechanism note that defines the market regime or suitability filter before testing.

H-B2 pre-registration and simulated-scale diagnostic completed with no new paid data. Eight pre-registered trials across $10k/$25k and $5/$10/$15/$20 wings found zero scenarios with both positive total and OOS implementable PnL. Formal falsification is deferred until MinTRL_falsify review; no deployment or paper-trading edge claim is allowed.

User challenged the original question: capped put ratio spreads should not be judged as if they must work every day. H-B2 stays parked for the current grid, but any future reopening must first state the real hypothesis: under which volatility/skew/regime/suitability conditions should this structure earn premium without unacceptable tail risk? No new H-B2 effort should run until that mechanism and filter are pre-registered.

hypotheses.statement: A signed-OI gamma proxy aggregated from OPRA open interest and self-computed Greeks carries economic signal: high positive proxy days should exhibit attenuated realized intraday volatility.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Positive aggregate dealer-style gamma exposure can damp realized volatility through hedging flows, while negative gamma can amplify moves. Current data supports only proxy validity, not true dealer inventory claims.
testable_predictions: Proxy terciles show monotonic or sign-consistent realized variance differences.
The relation survives across at least 10 dates and at least 3 regimes.
Coverage passes pre-registered v2 gates while v1 fail remains permanent.
validation_criteria: Issue GAMMA_AGGREGATION_VALIDATION_POLICY.md v2 before rerun.
Report raw-row and bucket-weighted coverage side by side.
Pass timestamp discipline, stability, economic-sign validation, and search-log gates.
falsification_criteria: No monotonic or sign-consistent relation between proxy tercile and realized 10:00-15:45 SPY variance across the pre-registered multi-regime date set.
required_data: Pre-registered 12-date regime set.
OPRA statistics/OI days for the 12-date set.
Existing quote and SPY bar cache for those dates.

hypotheses.statement: LLM sentiment, market-impact, and volatility-relevance scores over timestamp-clean pre-entry news are stable across repeated calls/prompt families and add information beyond VIX plus macro calendar.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Language models may compress qualitative news context into continuous sentiment/impact/volatility scores that deterministic calendars cannot capture, but only if stability and contamination controls pass first.
testable_predictions: Repeated calls have acceptable dispersion for the selected prompt family.
Masking/anonymization reduces event memorization without destroying signal.
Scores correlate with same-day realized volatility or adverse tails after controlling for VIX and macro calendar.
validation_criteria: Use real timestamp-clean news cases only.
Compare prompt-template families and masking policies.
Run 5-call stability where cost allows.
Record model id, cutoff, document timestamps, fetch window, masking policy, and contamination probe.
falsification_criteria: Real news cases remain unavailable.
Parse validity is unreliable.
Repeated-call dispersion is too high.
Scores add no information beyond VIX/macro baselines.
required_data: Real timestamp-clean pre-entry news archive.
Deterministic VIX and macro calendar baseline features.

evidence.conclusion: เธขเธฑเธเธชเธฃเธธเธเนเธกเนเนเธ”เน
evidence.notes: Design/scaffold evidence only.

evidence.conclusion: เธขเธฑเธเธชเธฃเธธเธเนเธกเนเนเธ”เน

hypotheses.statement: LLM prompts fed only chronological price/quote context may improve exit timing versus fixed TP/SL rules.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: A model might summarize intraday price/quote context into a dynamic exit assessment, but this must be separated from H-L1 and tested against strict chronological controls.
testable_predictions: Parse validity stays above the pre-registered threshold.
Any improvement survives ablation against fixed TP/SL grids.
No future same-day aggregate or post-decision data enters the prompt.
validation_criteria: Separate pre-registration.
Strict timestamp controls.
Search log and cost guard.
No mixing with news/sentiment prompts.
falsification_criteria: Parse validity is below threshold.
Chronological controls cannot be proven.
Cost or latency makes the branch impractical.
required_data: Chronological price and quote context at decision timestamps.
A design document before live LLM calls.

evidence.conclusion: เธขเธฑเธเธชเธฃเธธเธเนเธกเนเนเธ”เน
evidence.notes: H-L2 dormant design artifact. It separates price-input exit assistance from H-L1 news/sentiment and forbids future same-day data.

decision_log.notes: Dormant until H-L1 measurement validity is designed and news cases exist.
H-L2 price-input design is complete as E0 scaffold evidence. No live calls are allowed until a chronological case manifest, parser/simulator plumbing, and cost guard are ready.

hypotheses.statement: A text filter trained on timestamp-clean SPY market outcomes after the decision time reduces strategy tail loss and adds information beyond semantic sentiment plus VIX and macro-calendar controls.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Text labels tied to subsequent market outcomes may capture trade-relevant downside risk that generic positive/negative tone misses. The SMARTyBERT paper motivates the construct, but its stock-level next-day labels and validation-tuned thresholds are not directly transferable to same-day SPY 0DTE.
testable_predictions: A market-outcome-trained score predicts post-decision SPY downside or adverse excursion better than a semantic sentiment score on untouched chronological data.
The market-based filter improves ES99 and worst-day loss relative to quantitative-only and semantic-filter controls without collapsing active-trade count below an acceptance-grade sample.
Any strategy benefit survives implementable costs, DSR adjustment, big-day dependency checks, and separate Sub-System A and Sub-System B ablations.
validation_criteria: Use real news records with publication and availability timestamps before the applicable strategy decision time.
Define SPY-specific post-decision labels using chronological train/validation/test splits and no random shuffling.
Compare quantitative-only, semantic-only, market-based-only, quantitative-plus-semantic, and quantitative-plus-market-based arms.
Keep the 09:35 Sub-System A entry test separate from any 10:00 delayed-entry branch.
Freeze aggregation thresholds on validation data; do not import the paper's 0.5 and 2.0 thresholds as SPY defaults.
Report active trades, implementable PnL, ES99 uncertainty, MinTRL/PSR for Sharpe, DSR trial count, and big-day dependency.
falsification_criteria: Market-based scores add no predictive information beyond semantic sentiment, VIX, and macro-calendar controls.
Tail-risk improvement disappears on untouched chronological data or after implementable costs.
The filter leaves too few tail observations or active trades for a credible conclusion.
Timestamp cleanliness, label chronology, or source licensing cannot be demonstrated.
required_data: Real timestamp-clean pre-entry news with publication and availability timestamps.
Chronological SPY post-decision returns and adverse-excursion targets.
Semantic sentiment benchmark and deterministic VIX/macro controls.
Separate implementable strategy outcomes for Sub-System A and Sub-System B.

mintrl_falsify.reason: No real timestamp-clean news archive or SPY-specific market-label design exists yet; ES99 also requires a separate tail-sample uncertainty plan.

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
evidence.notes: Hypothesis registration and guardrail design only; no model training or strategy result exists.

decision_log.notes: Registered from the user-proposed market-based versus semantic filter experiment as a SPY-specific hypothesis inspired by SMARTyBERT, not a direct replication.
